decisions.title: User authentication via external SSO only
decisions.detail: Users will log in exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal.

evidence.quote: Users will log in exclusively via SSO through the external Portal.

decisions.title: System will not implement internal login/registration
decisions.detail: The system will not include its own login or registration functionality.

evidence.quote: This system will not implement its own login or registration functionality.

decisions.title: Prioritize Photo upload feature
decisions.detail: Photo upload is identified as a main feature to prioritize for implementation.

evidence.quote: Two main features to prioritize: 1. Photo upload

decisions.title: Prioritize Output modeling feature
decisions.detail: Output modeling is identified as a main feature to prioritize for implementation.

evidence.quote: Two main features to prioritize: 2. Output modeling

decisions.title: Sidebar to distinguish Album and Project folder types
decisions.detail: The user interface sidebar must differentiate between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, managing photos and modeling data separately.

evidence.quote: The sidebar needs to distinguish between two folder types — "Album" and "Project" — managing photos and modeling data separately

decisions.title: Album must support photo management
decisions.detail: The Album functionality must allow users to add, delete, and search photos.

evidence.quote: The Album must support adding, deleting, and searching photos

decisions.title: Modeling progress displayed as a progress bar
decisions.detail: Modeling progress must be visually represented as a progress bar, indicating the status of each stage (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).

evidence.quote: Modeling progress must be shown as a progress bar, displaying the status of each stage (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation, etc.)

decisions.title: Project creation allows photo selection from list
decisions.detail: When creating a new project, users must be able to select photos from an existing photo list to import them into the project folder.

evidence.quote: When creating a project, users can select photos from the photo list to import into the project folder

decisions.title: Project sharing feature (future version)
decisions.detail: In a future version, project folders will support designating other users to jointly view modeling results. Albums will remain private to the owner and not support multi-user access.

evidence.quote: Project sharing: A project folder can designate other users to jointly view the modeling results; Albums are private to the owner only and do not support multi-user access
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

decisions.title: Resumable uploads feature (future version)
decisions.detail: In a future version, if an upload is interrupted, its progress must be preserved via a caching mechanism, enabling resume-from-breakpoint functionality.

evidence.quote: Resumable uploads: If an upload is interrupted, upload progress is preserved via a caching mechanism, supporting resume-from-breakpoint
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

decisions.title: Storage quota management feature (future version)
decisions.detail: In a future version, the user's personal page will display used and remaining storage. Each user's photo-upload limit will be dynamically adjustable based on system settings and permissions.

evidence.quote: Storage quota management: The user's personal page displays used and remaining storage; each user's photo-upload limit can be dynamically adjusted based on system settings and permissions
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

decisions.title: AT error handling mechanism (future version)
decisions.detail: In a future version, a mechanism must be provided to adjust parameters and re-run AT computation when it fails.

evidence.quote: AT error handling: When AT computation fails, provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

decisions.title: Expert mode feature (future version)
decisions.detail: In a future version, after photo upload, the system will distinguish between 'Normal Mode' (default parameters) and 'Expert Mode' (freely adjustable parameters).

evidence.quote: Expert mode: After photo upload, distinguish between "Normal Mode" (default parameters) and "Expert Mode" (freely adjustable parameters)
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

decisions.title: V1 to implement Normal Mode only for parameter adjustment
decisions.detail: For the V1 implementation, only 'Normal Mode' with default parameters will be available, while 'Expert Mode' is deferred.

evidence.quote: V1 will implement Normal Mode only
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Advanced Features (Future Version Planning)

implementation_scope: Design and implement UI sidebar components to differentiate 'Album' and 'Project' folder types, facilitating separate management of photos and modeling data (D005).
Develop CRUD operations (add, delete, search) for photos within 'Album' folders (D006).
Integrate a visual progress bar component for displaying modeling stages (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation) (D007).
Implement the project creation workflow, allowing users to select and import photos from an existing list into new project folders (D008).
Ensure modeling parameter adjustment supports 'Normal Mode' with default parameters only for V1 (D014).

constraints.text: User authentication must exclusively use an external Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal.
The system must not implement its own internal login or registration functionality.

open_technical_questions: What is the specific API/protocol for integrating with the external SSO provider (e.g., OIDC, SAML), and what user attributes will be passed?
What is the data model for 'Album' and 'Project' folders, and how will photo/modeling data be structured and stored within them?
What is the backend API for photo upload, and what are the requirements for photo storage (e.g., cloud storage, local file system, metadata)?
What are the API contracts and communication mechanisms for interacting with the backend modeling service (e.g., initiating computation, polling for progress, fetching results)?
What are the specific stages and associated status messages required for the modeling progress bar UI (D007)?
How is the 'existing photo list' generated and presented during project creation, and what backend endpoints support this functionality?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts to access the system, When the user is redirected to the external SSO portal, Then the user must successfully log in via SSO to access the system.
Given a user attempts to register or log in directly within the system, When the system is accessed, Then no internal login or registration forms are presented.
Given a user is in an Album, When the user initiates a photo upload, Then the photo is successfully added to the Album.
Given a user has photos in an Album, When the user selects a photo and chooses to delete it, Then the photo is permanently removed from the Album.
Given a user has multiple photos in an Album, When the user uses the search functionality, Then photos matching the criteria are displayed.
Given a user creates a new Project, When the user is prompted to add photos, Then the user can select photos from an existing list and import them into the Project.

Given a modeling process is initiated, When the modeling process progresses through stages (e.g., AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation), Then a visual progress bar accurately displays the current stage and overall status.
Given the user is viewing the sidebar, When folder types are displayed, Then 'Album' type folders are visually distinguishable from 'Project' type folders.
Given a user selects an 'Album' folder, When managing content, Then only photo-related data can be stored and managed.
Given a user selects a 'Project' folder, When managing content, Then only modeling-related data can be stored and managed.

Given a user accesses the parameter adjustment interface for modeling, When parameters are presented, Then only 'Normal Mode' default parameters are available, and 'Expert Mode' options are not accessible.
test_scenarios: Verify a user can successfully log in to the system via the external SSO portal.
Verify the system does not present any internal login or registration forms to the user.
Verify a single photo can be uploaded successfully to an Album.
Verify multiple photos can be uploaded successfully to an Album.
Verify an uploaded photo can be deleted from an Album.
Verify searching for an existing photo by its name returns the correct results.
Verify creating a new Project allows selecting and importing photos from the existing photo list.
Verify that an initiated modeling process displays a progress bar that updates through its stages (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation).
Verify 'Album' folders and 'Project' folders are clearly differentiated in the sidebar.
Verify that when viewing an 'Album' folder, only photo management options are available.
Verify that when viewing a 'Project' folder, only modeling data management options are available.
Verify that when accessing modeling parameter adjustments, only 'Normal Mode' default parameters are configurable.
edge_cases: Attempt to log in via SSO when the external SSO portal is unavailable.
User's SSO session expires while actively using the system.
Attempt to upload unsupported file types (e.g., text files, videos) to an Album.
Attempt to upload a very large number of photos to an Album simultaneously.
Search for a photo using an empty search string or special characters.
Attempt to delete a photo that is currently involved in an active modeling process (if applicable).
Initiate a modeling process that completes exceptionally quickly or slowly.
Observe the progress bar's behavior if a modeling stage fails or becomes stuck (before V1 error handling).
Verify no 'Album' or 'Project' folders are displayed in the sidebar if the user has none.
Verify 'Expert Mode' settings or interfaces are not accessible through direct URLs or developer tools in V1.
unresolved_behaviors: What specific attributes (e.g., name, date, tags) are supported for searching photos within an Album?
What are the supported photo file formats and maximum individual file size for photo uploads?
Is there a maximum number of photos that can be stored in an Album or imported into a Project?
How is the 'existing photo list' populated when creating a Project? Does it aggregate photos from all Albums, or a specific user's photo pool?
What is the expected visual distinction (e.g., icon, label, color) between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types in the sidebar?
How does the system handle an external SSO authentication failure (e.g., invalid credentials, network issue) and communicate this to the user?

Decisions D009 (Project sharing), D010 (Resumable uploads), D011 (Storage quota management), D012 (AT error handling), and D013 (Expert mode) are explicitly designated for future versions and will not be implemented in V1. Testing will focus on verifying their absence and ensuring no related functionality is exposed.

decision_history: It was decided that user authentication will exclusively rely on an external Single Sign-On (SSO) through the external Portal. Consequently, the system will not implement any internal login or registration functionality (D001, D002).
The primary features to prioritize for initial implementation are Photo upload and Output modeling (D003, D004).

The user interface sidebar must clearly differentiate between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types to manage photos and modeling data separately. 'Album' functionality will include adding, deleting, and searching photos (D005, D006).
Modeling progress will be visually displayed as a progress bar, showing the status of each stage (D007).
Users will be able to select existing photos from a list when creating a new project to import them into the project folder (D008).

Several advanced features, including Project sharing, Resumable uploads, Storage quota management, AT error handling, and Expert mode, are explicitly deferred to future versions of the system (D009, D010, D011, D012, D013).
For the V1 implementation, only 'Normal Mode' with default parameters for photo processing will be available; 'Expert Mode' is deferred to a future version (D014).
dependencies: External Portal/SSO system: The project is entirely dependent on an external SSO system for user authentication. This is a critical cross-system dependency for user access (Confirmed, D001, D002).

Photo list availability: Project creation relies on users selecting photos from an existing photo list (Confirmed, D008). This implies a dependency on the 'Album' photo management feature (D006) to populate this list.
risks: External SSO integration risk: Reliance on an external SSO for all authentication introduces integration complexity and potential delays if the external system's team is unresponsive or if API contracts are unclear. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High - project blocker).

Scope creep expectation management: While future features (Project sharing, Resumable uploads, Storage quota, AT error handling, Expert mode) are deferred, there's a risk of stakeholders expecting these sooner than planned if not clearly communicated. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium - rework/dissatisfaction).
alignment_gaps: None explicitly identified based on confirmed decisions. The meeting seems to have clearly scoped V1 vs. future versions.
open_questions: High: What are the exact integration details and API specifications for the external SSO system? Who is the technical contact for the external Portal/SSO team, and what is their availability/SLA for integration support? (Ownership: PM - cross-team dependency timing/ownership)
Medium: What are the specific acceptance criteria for 'Normal Mode' parameter defaults and how will these be validated? (Ownership: PM/QA - requirements/acceptance-criteria gaps)

system_requirements: The system SHALL authenticate users exclusively via Single Sign-On (SSO) through an external Portal (D001).
The user interface sidebar SHALL visually differentiate between 'Album' and 'Project' folder types (D005).
The system SHALL manage photos and modeling data separately based on 'Album' and 'Project' folder types (D005).
The Album functionality SHALL allow users to add photos (D006).
The Album functionality SHALL allow users to delete photos (D006).
The Album functionality SHALL allow users to search photos (D006).
The system SHALL visually represent modeling progress as a progress bar (D007).
The progress bar SHALL indicate the status of each modeling stage, including AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation (D007).
When creating a new project, users SHALL be able to select photos from an existing photo list to import them into the project folder (D008).
In a future version, Project folders SHALL support designating other users to jointly view modeling results (D009).
In a future version, Album folders SHALL remain private to the owner and MUST NOT support multi-user access (D009).
In a future version, the system SHALL preserve upload progress via a caching mechanism if an upload is interrupted (D010).
In a future version, the system SHALL enable resume-from-breakpoint functionality for interrupted uploads (D010).
In a future version, the user's personal page SHALL display used and remaining storage (D011).
In a future version, the system SHALL dynamically adjust each user's photo-upload limit based on system settings and permissions (D011).
In a future version, the system SHALL provide a mechanism to adjust parameters and re-run AT computation when it fails (D012).
In a future version, after photo upload, the system SHALL distinguish between 'Normal Mode' and 'Expert Mode' (D013).
In a future version, 'Normal Mode' SHALL utilize default parameters (D013).
In a future version, 'Expert Mode' SHALL allow freely adjustable parameters (D013).
data_flows: User authentication credentials from the external Portal SSO to the system (D001).
User identity and authorization data from the external Portal SSO to the system (inferred from D001).
Photo data from the user interface to the system's Album storage (D003, D006).
Photo data from the system's photo list to a Project folder upon user selection (D008).
Modeling progress status updates from the modeling engine to the user interface (D007).
Modeling input data (photos) from Project folders to the modeling engine (inferred from D004).
Modeling output data from the modeling engine to Project folders (D004, D005).
interface_contracts: External SSO Interface: The system SHALL integrate with the external Portal's SSO service for user authentication. This requires a defined protocol (e.g., OIDC, SAML) and a contract for user attribute exchange (D001).
Modeling Progress API: The system's UI will consume an API providing real-time status updates for modeling stages (AT computing, point cloud generation, model generation) (D007).
Photo Management API: The system will expose APIs for adding, deleting, and searching photos within Albums (D006).
Project Photo Import API: The system will expose an API to select and import photos from an existing photo list into a Project folder (D008).

constraints.text: The system MUST NOT implement its own internal login or registration functionality (D002).

architectural_decisions.text: The system architecture for V1 SHALL prioritize the Photo upload feature (D003).
The system architecture for V1 SHALL prioritize the Output modeling feature (D004).
For V1 implementation, only 'Normal Mode' with default parameters for photo processing will be available; 'Expert Mode' is deferred (D014).

open_analysis_questions: What specific SSO protocol (e.g., OIDC, SAML) will be used for integration with the external Portal, and what user attributes will be provided by the SSO service upon successful authentication (D001)?
What specific metadata fields or indexing capabilities are required for the 'search photos' functionality within Albums (D006)?
What is the source and management mechanism for the 'existing photo list' from which photos are selected during project creation (D008)?
What are the precise data structures and storage mechanisms that differentiate 'photos' managed in Albums from 'modeling data' managed in Projects (D005)?
What are the exact stages and associated statuses for 'AT computing, point cloud generation, and model generation' that need to be reported for the progress bar (D007)?

stakeholder.business_summary: We have confirmed the core features for the initial release, prioritizing photo upload and output modeling. User login will be exclusively managed through our existing external portal, simplifying access and development. We also outlined plans for advanced features to be introduced in future versions.
key_impact: Users will have a consistent and familiar login experience using the external portal, without needing new credentials.
The initial product will focus on essential capabilities: uploading photos, organizing them into Albums, and generating modeling outputs.
A clear distinction between personal photo Albums and collaborative Project folders will help users manage their data effectively.
Future updates will introduce valuable features like project sharing, resumable uploads, and advanced modeling controls, enhancing user experience and system capabilities over time.
major_unresolved_questions: What is the timeline and budget allocation for developing the advanced features planned for future versions?
Are there any specific business objectives or success metrics tied to the prioritization of photo upload and output modeling features for the first version?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions and show no signs of hallucination. The quality of the outputs is high, with appropriate detail and language for each target audience. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.
